description: Operational skill catalog for the fabric-pi Pi profile. Tiers express HOW the primary routes a skill (load model), not just visibility. Every installed SKILL.md directory under .pi/skills/ appears in exactly one tier. The dispatch skill_policy (config.json) resolves briefs against this catalog.
changelog: v2: restructured tiers into operational semantics (kernel | mandatory-on-trigger | specialist | high-context); added per-skill triggers for the mandatory tier, intent bundles, and primary_only identification; reconciled catalog with shipped SKILL.md dirs (was missing 8).
v1: numeric tiers 1 (Essential) / 2 (On-Demand); incomplete coverage of shipped skills.

tier_semantics.kernel: Always-considered execution discipline. The primary considers these first for every task, but a kernel skill is still named in required_skills and loaded by the receiving role when relevant — it is never loaded implicitly.
tier_semantics.mandatory-on-trigger: Hard-precondition platform/domain skills. When the brief's trigger fires, the primary MUST attach the skill before spawn — skipping it risks correctness, safety, or billing footguns. Non-optional.
tier_semantics.specialist: Domain and process skills loaded on demand when the brief's domain matches. Discretionary; the primary attaches when relevant, matched against intent_bundles and per-skill triggers.
tier_semantics.high-context: Whole-codebase or large-context-window analysis. Too heavy for a bounded leaf worker; the primary routes these to the primary session or a large-model lane.

kernel.label: Kernel
kernel.load: considered first for every task; named in required_skills when relevant, not loaded implicitly
kernel.description: Small, general-purpose execution-discipline skills that shape how any role works. The primary considers these first for every task and names the relevant one(s) in required_skills; they are never loaded implicitly.
kernel.triggers: no explicit trigger; consider first for every task to shape execution discipline (validate at every layer, make small reversible changes, prove before claiming done)

mandatory-on-trigger.label: Mandatory on Trigger
mandatory-on-trigger.load: primary MUST attach when the trigger fires; non-optional
mandatory-on-trigger.description: Skills whose own contract declares a hard precondition (MUST load before X) tied to a specific technology or domain, where skipping risks correctness, safety, or billing footguns. The primary resolves these against the brief and attaches before spawn.

triggers.cloudflare: any Cloudflare Workers/KV/D1/R2/AI/Tunnel/WAF code or wrangler config
triggers.core-data-expert: Core Data schema, migrations, merge conflicts, CloudKit sync, or fetch/perf work
triggers.figma: user shares a Figma URL or references Figma files (requires API token)
triggers.frontend-design: building any web UI with a React-based framework (base implementation layer)
triggers.pdf-extract: extracting text/images/tables/metadata from a PDF file
triggers.playwright: any automated browser testing or end-to-end UX verification
triggers.polar: checkout, subscriptions, license keys, customer portal, or any Polar billing code
triggers.react-best-practices: writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js for performance
triggers.resend: transactional emails, React Email templates, email webhooks, or inbound handling
triggers.supabase: Supabase DB ops, edge functions, auth, storage, or project management
triggers.supabase-postgres-best-practices: Postgres queries, schema, indexes, or RLS policies in Supabase
triggers.webclaw: webfetch returns 403/bot-protection, or non-trivial crawling/batch page extraction

specialist.label: Specialist
specialist.load: on-demand when the brief's domain matches; discretionary
specialist.description: Domain, platform, and process skills. The primary attaches these when the brief matches an intent bundle or per-skill trigger; otherwise the role proceeds with defaults.
specialist.triggers: match brief domain to skill via intent_bundles and the skill's own description; attach when relevant. Several specialist skills are tagged primary_only.interactive (brainstorming, grill-me, grill-with-docs) and may not be routed to leaf workers.

high-context.label: High-Context
high-context.load: primary or large-model lane only; too heavy for a bounded leaf worker
high-context.description: Whole-codebase or large-context-window analysis skills. The primary routes these to the primary session or a large-model lane, never to a bounded leaf worker.
high-context.triggers: project-wide pattern analysis, structural review, or investigation exceeding typical leaf context; route to primary/large-model, not a bounded leaf

primary_only.description: Skills requiring operator dialogue or primary-session authority. The dispatch skill_policy denies the union of dispatch.leaf_excluded_skills.skills and primary_only.interactive from any subagent (leaf) brief and reroutes them to the primary session.

reason_by_skill.brainstorming: collaborative questioning and incremental approval require operator dialogue; leaf workers cannot ask the operator
reason_by_skill.grill-me: interrogates an idea/ADR/PRD/spec with the operator until it survives scrutiny
reason_by_skill.grill-with-docs: stress-tests a plan against project language and documented decisions via operator dialogue

primary_only.leaf_excluded_ref: config.dispatch.leaf_excluded_skills.skills is the operator-curated hard-exclusion subset (grill-me, grill-with-docs) pinned by tests/profile-contracts.test.mjs; this field additionally identifies brainstorming as a primary-only interactive skill surfaced during catalog expansion. The skill_policy unions both for enforcement.

supabase-backend.when: DB queries, RLS, edge functions, auth, storage, or Postgres perf in Supabase

react-premium-ui.when: building React/Next.js UI; swap design-taste-frontend for high-end-visual-design, minimalist-ui, or industrial-brutalist-ui per the requested aesthetic

aesthetic-overlay.when: choosing a visual aesthetic; exactly one overlay loads INSTEAD OF the others

browser-verify.when: verify DOM/console/network/screenshots or end-to-end flows; playwright is mandatory for automated tests

quality-gate.when: before claiming implementation complete, or after subagent work, before merge

debug-trace.when: tests fail, behavior is unexpected, or repeated fix attempts have not worked

tdd.when: implementing a feature or bugfix with testable behavior

ios-platform.when: SwiftUI, Swift concurrency, or Core Data work on iOS/macOS

design-from-source.when: a Figma URL or mockup/wireframe is being converted to code

large-codebase-analysis.when: project-wide patterns or structural review exceeding typical context; route to primary/large-model

api-contract.when: designing or evolving REST/GraphQL APIs, SDK boundaries, or planning breaking changes
